summary: Scanned Lane C immersive/player/environment paths plus current lane state/findings. Revalidated the existing immersive-control wiring around screen-size handling and found one new high-confidence bug: the immersive "Change environment" control posts a notification, but PlayerView only refreshes the asset list instead of presenting any picker or switch flow, so the control is effectively inert.

newFindings.title: Immersive "Change environment" control never opens a picker or switches environments
newFindings.why_it_is_a_bug: The immersive controls expose a dedicated environment-switch button, but the only handler wired in PlayerView for that notification just reloads the environment asset list. It never toggles `isShowingEnvironmentPicker`, never presents any picker sheet, and never performs a switch. From the user's perspective, the button does nothing.
newFindings.trigger_or_repro: Start playback in an immersive environment, show the floating immersive controls, and tap the mountain-shaped "Change environment" button. `ImmersivePlayerControlsView` posts `.immersiveControlRequestEnvironmentSwitch`, and `PlayerView`'s `ImmersiveControlHandlers` closure runs only `Task { await loadEnvironmentAssets() }`. No picker appears and the environment does not change.
newFindings.impact: Users inside immersive playback cannot actually switch environments from the advertised in-space control, forcing them to leave the immersive flow and use other UI instead. The control is misleading and effectively broken.
newFindings.evidence: `ImmersivePlayerControlsView.secondaryControlsRow` labels the button as "Change environment" and posts `.immersiveControlRequestEnvironmentSwitch`. In `PlayerView`, the attached `ImmersiveControlHandlers` maps `onRequestEnvironmentSwitch` to `{ Task { await loadEnvironmentAssets() } }` only. Unlike the window menu/button paths elsewhere in `PlayerView`, this handler never sets `isShowingEnvironmentPicker = true` or calls `openEnvironment(...)`.
